TIME: 6:00 PM TO 8:30 PM

CALL TO ORDER:
The meeting was called to order by Commission Chairperson Martha G. Halloway at 6:02 PM. The Chairperson read the notice of meeting in accordance with the Open Meeting Act. The Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag of the United States was recited by all attendees.

ROLL CALL:
Present and voting were:
1. Martha G. Halloway, Chairperson
2. David L. Chen, Vice-Chairperson
3. Sarah J. Miller, Member
4. Robert T. Williams, Member
5. Jennifer K. O’Connell, Member

Absent: None.
Staff Present:
1. Thomas R. Evans, Director of Parks and Recreation
2. Amanda S. Lewis, Assistant Director
3. Kevin P. Murphy, City Clerk
4. Linda M. Foster, Administrative Assistant
5. James H. Wilson, Chief Financial Officer

The quorum was established at 6:05 PM. The meeting proceeded with the standard order of business.

AGENDA ITEM 1: APPROVAL OF PREVIOUS MINUTES
The minutes from the previous regular meeting held on June 12, 2024, were distributed to all members. A motion to approve the minutes as written was made by Vice-Chairperson David L. Chen and seconded by Member Sarah J. Miller.
Motion 2024-07-10-A: Approval of June 12, 2024 Minutes.
Vote Count: 5-0.
Result: Approved.
The minutes were entered into the official record.

AGENDA ITEM 2: INFRASTRUCTURE AUDIT REVIEW
Director Thomas R. Evans presented the Infrastructure Audit Report for the fiscal year 2024. The report detailed the results of the annual facility safety inspections conducted by the City Engineering Division between May 1, 2024, and June 30, 2024. The inspection covered all public parks, recreation centers, and athletic complexes under the jurisdiction of the Parks and Recreation Commission.

The report identified three (3) specific structures requiring immediate repairs to maintain compliance with the City Safety Code Section 14.2. The structures were listed as follows:
1. Structure A: Oakhaven Community Center, North Wing Roof Truss System. Inspection Date: May 15, 2024. Condition Rating: Critical. Defect: Corrosion detected on load-bearing steel supports.
2. Structure B: Westside Athletic Complex, Dugout Facility No. 2. Inspection Date: May 22, 2024. Condition Rating: High Risk. Defect: Structural instability in concrete foundation near drainage outlet.
3. Structure C: Riverside Park Playground Equipment, Climbing Tower Unit 4. Inspection Date: June 5, 2024. Condition Rating: Moderate to High Risk. Defect: Degradation of safety surfacing material and loose anchoring bolts.

Director Evans recommended immediate repairs for all three structures to ensure public safety and prevent liability. The staff recommendation included a budget allocation of $185,000 for the immediate repairs. The funds were to be drawn from the Capital Improvement Reserve Fund.

Commissioner Robert T. Williams requested clarification on the timeline for the repairs. Director Evans stated that the City Engineering Division could begin work within 14 days of contract approval. Commissioner Williams asked if the repairs would require the closure of the affected facilities. Director Evans responded that Structure C (Playground Equipment) would require closure during the anchoring repair process, estimated to take three (3) days. Structure A and Structure B could be repaired with partial access to the facilities.

Commissioner Jennifer K. O’Connell asked about the long-term maintenance plan for the affected structures. Assistant Director Amanda S. Lewis provided a summary of the preventative maintenance schedule, noting that the current inspection cycle had been extended by two years due to budget constraints in the prior fiscal year. The new recommendation restored the standard one-year inspection cycle.

A motion to approve the staff recommendation for immediate repairs to the three identified structures was made by Vice-Chairperson David L. Chen and seconded by Member Sarah J. Miller.
Motion 2024-07-10-B: Approval of Infrastructure Audit Repairs.
Text: To approve the staff recommendation for immediate repairs to 3 structures identified in the Infrastructure Audit Report dated July 10, 2024.
Vote Count: 5-0.
Result: Approved.
The motion carried unanimously. The City Clerk recorded the vote count as 5-0.

AGENDA ITEM 3: BUDGET REVIEW AND QUARTERLY REPORTS
The Chief Financial Officer, James H. Wilson, presented the Quarterly Financial Report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2024. The report indicated that the Parks and Recreation Bureau had spent 45% of the allocated budget for operations and 30% of the allocated budget for capital improvements. The variance from the projected expenditure was within the acceptable margin of 5%.

Commissioner Sarah J. Miller inquired about the allocation for the upcoming summer programming. Director Evans confirmed that the summer programming budget remained stable at $320,000. The funds cover youth sports leagues, senior fitness classes, and special events at the city parks.

AGENDA ITEM 4: NEW BUSINESS
There was no new business presented.

AGENDA ITEM 5: ADJOURNMENT
The meeting was adjourned at 8:25 PM. The next regular meeting of the Parks and Recreation Commission is scheduled for August 14, 2024, at 6:00 PM.

CERTIFICATION:
I, Kevin P. Murphy, City Clerk, do hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct copy of the minutes of the Parks and Recreation Commission meeting held on July 10, 2024. The vote count was recorded as 5-0.

Signed:
Kevin P. Murphy, City Clerk
Date: July 15, 2024

DOCUMENT 2: CONTRACT AGREEMENT
CONTRACT NUMBER: CW-2024-0805-IR
EFFECTIVE DATE: AUGUST 5, 2024
TERMINATION DATE: AUGUST 4, 2025

THIS AGREEMENT (the "Contract") is made and entered into by and between the CITY OF OAKHAVEN (hereinafter referred to as the "City"), a municipal corporation organized under the laws of the State of Oakhaven, and WATERWISE SOLUTIONS, LLC (hereinafter referred to as the "Contractor"), a limited liability company organized under the laws of the State of Oakhaven.

RECITALS:
WHEREAS, the City desires to maintain the irrigation systems located within City parks and recreational facilities; and
WHEREAS, the Contractor represents that it possesses the necessary expertise, equipment, and personnel to perform the maintenance services described herein; and
WHEREAS, the City has determined that the Contractor is qualified to perform the services pursuant to the Request for Proposal issued on May 1, 2024.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

SECTION 1: SCOPE OF SERVICES
The Contractor shall provide maintenance services for the irrigation systems across all City parks. The Scope of Work includes, but is not limited to:
1.1 Sensor Installation: Installation of moisture sensors at key irrigation zones within Oakhaven Community Park, Riverside Park, and Westside Athletic Complex.
1.2 Leak Detection: Conducting bi-weekly inspections for leak detection throughout the system.
1.3 System Optimization: Adjusting water pressure and flow rates based on sensor data and seasonal weather patterns.
1.4 Reporting: Submission of monthly reports detailing water usage, system status, and maintenance actions taken.
1.5 Emergency Response: Provision of 24-hour emergency response for major irrigation failures.

SECTION 2: COMPENSATION
In consideration for the performance of the Services, the City shall pay the Contractor a total Value of $25,000 (Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars).
2.1 Payment Schedule: Payment shall be made in four (4) equal installments of $6,250.00.
2.2 First Payment: Due upon execution of this Contract.
2.3 Subsequent Payments: Due on the first day of each subsequent quarter.
2.4 Final Payment: Due upon completion of all services and submission of the final report.

SECTION 3: TERM AND TERMINATION
3.1 Term: This Contract shall commence on August 5, 2024, and shall terminate on August 4, 2025, unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Section.
3.2 Termination for Cause: The City may terminate this Contract for cause if the Contractor fails to perform the Scope of Services.
3.3 Termination for Convenience: The City may terminate this Contract for convenience upon thirty (30) days written notice.

SECTION 4: INDEMNIFICATION AND INSURANCE
4.1 Insurance: The Contractor shall maintain general liability insurance with a minimum coverage of $1,000,000 per occurrence.
4.2 Indemnification: The Contractor agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the City from any claims arising out of the Contractor’s negligence.

SECTION 5: COMPLIANCE WITH LAWS
The Contractor shall comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, ordinances, and regulations, including but not limited to environmental protection standards regarding water usage.

SECTION 6: DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Any disputes arising out of this Contract shall be resolved through mediation in the County of Oakhaven. If mediation is unsuccessful, the dispute shall be resolved in the courts of the State of Oakhaven.

SECTION 7: SIGNATURES
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Contract as of the Effective Date.

DOCUMENT 4: INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMO
MEMORANDUM
TO: Director of Parks and Recreation, Thomas R. Evans
FROM: Director of Transportation, Linda M. Foster
CC: City Engineer, James H. Wilson
DATE: SEPTEMBER 10, 2024
SUBJECT: COORDINATION BETWEEN PARKS AND TRANSPORTATION
REF: PROJECT ID: BIKE-2024-09

PURPOSE:
The purpose of this memorandum is to establish schedule alignment for bike lane construction near park entrance. This coordination is necessary to minimize disruption to park visitors and ensure public safety during construction activities.

BACKGROUND:
The Transportation Department has identified a need to upgrade the bike lane infrastructure connecting Main Street to the Westside Athletic Complex. The proposed route passes directly adjacent to the park entrance. The Parks and Recreation Department manages the pedestrian access points and landscaping in this area.

TECHNICAL DETAILS:
1. Construction Location: 50-foot section of bike lane on the east side of the park entrance.
2. Construction Duration: Estimated 10 working days, starting September 20, 2024.
3. Traffic Control Plan: A traffic control plan has been prepared by the City Engineer. The plan includes lane closures, detour signage, and pedestrian rerouting.
4. City Engineer Approval: The Traffic Control Plan was approved by the City Engineer on September 5, 2024.

COORDINATION REQUIREMENTS:
1. Schedule Alignment: Construction activities must cease between 6:00 PM and 8:00 PM to accommodate evening park usage.
2. Access Maintenance: Pedestrian access to the park must remain open at all times. A temporary walkway will be installed during construction.
3. Noise Control: Construction equipment must adhere to the City Noise Ordinance limits.
4. Signage: Parks Department signage must be updated to reflect the temporary detour.

APPROVAL STATUS:
The Parks and Recreation Department acknowledges receipt of this memo. The Department agrees to the schedule alignment. The Transportation Department will coordinate with the Parks Department to post signage one week prior to construction.

PROJECT: IRRIGATION SYSTEM MAINTENANCE
DOCUMENT TYPE: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT SUMMARY
DATE: AUGUST 15, 2024
PREPARED BY: Environmental Protection Division

PROJECT TITLE: WATERWISE SOLUTIONS IRRIGATION MAINTENANCE CONTRACT
PARCEL ID: 04-02-100-055
LOCATION: OAKHAVEN COMMUNITY PARK AND SURROUNDING PARKS

SCOPE OF REVIEW:
This summary reviews the potential environmental impacts of the Maintenance Services Contract awarded to WaterWise Solutions. The scope includes sensor installation and leak detection.

ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS:
1. Water Usage: The installation of moisture sensors is designed to optimize water usage. The expected outcome is a reduction in water waste by approximately 10%.
2. Chemical Usage: No chemical treatments are involved in the maintenance scope. The work is mechanical and electronic.
3. Soil Disturbance: Sensor installation requires minor excavation. The excavation is limited to 12 inches depth.
4. Noise: Construction noise will be limited to daytime hours.
5. Waste: Old sensors will be recycled in accordance with City Waste Management Policy.

MITIGATION MEASURES:
1. Excavation will be backfilled immediately after installation.
2. Work will be conducted outside of bird nesting seasons where possible.
3. Water runoff from excavation will be contained to prevent sediment entry into storm drains.

CONCLUSION:
The proposed action does not have a significant adverse effect on the environment. The project complies with the City Environmental Protection Ordinance. No further environmental review is required.

APPROVED BY:
Dr. Emily R. Carter, Environmental Protection Officer

V. ADMINISTRATIVE NOTES

This request has been logged into the Millbrook Public Records Management System (MPRMS) under the "Escalation" phase of the current fiscal year's transparency initiative. The request targets specific zoning activities and financial disclosures related to the River Road corridor. The requestor has indicated an intent to publish findings in the Millbrook Herald.

The request is categorized as "High Priority" due to the proximity of the requested property records to a pending zoning variance application currently under review by the Zoning Board of Appeals. The request for campaign finance records is linked to the target entities' potential involvement in the aforementioned zoning proceedings.

The Millbrook Ethics Commission has been notified of the request. A preliminary review of the records suggests a high volume of data regarding property transfers in the specified radius. The County Recorder's Office has been contacted to facilitate the retrieval of deed records.

The request includes a specific inquiry into "LLC ownership connections." This requires cross-referencing business license data with property tax rolls. The FOIA Clerk has initiated a query against the State Business Registry database.

VI. RESPONSE FORMAT

The requested records will be provided in digital format (PDF) via secure email transmission to the address listed above. A physical copy will be filed in Room 101 of the FOIA Office for public inspection. The digital copy will be available for download via the Millbrook Public Records Portal within 24 hours of the final response generation.

The response will include:
1. A cover letter detailing the scope of the search and the documents produced.
2. Redacted copies of any documents where exemptions under State Statute 12.4.1 (c)(2) regarding personal privacy might apply (though none are anticipated for campaign finance and public property records).
3. A statement regarding the completeness of the search.
4. Contact information for the FOIA Clerk for any follow-up inquiries.

SECTION C: PROJECT SCOPE AND DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Project Title: North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II
Project Type: Infrastructure Replacement and Modernization
Location: North District Water Treatment Plant, Parcel NW-8892.

Detailed Description of Work:
The Applicant requests approval for construction permits to facilitate the replacement of aging high-volume pumping infrastructure and the installation of advanced water sterilization technology. The proposed scope of work is limited to the existing utility footprint and does not involve the expansion of the facility's building envelope beyond the designated equipment pad.

1. High-Volume Pump Replacement:
   The Applicant proposes the removal and disposal of three existing centrifugal pumps, manufactured in 1998, which have exceeded their operational lifecycle. These pumps are currently designated as Units 1, 2, and 3 in the main pump house.
   The proposed replacement involves the installation of three new high-efficiency, variable-frequency drive (VFD) pumps. The new units are specified to provide increased flow capacity while maintaining the existing discharge pressure requirements.
   Demolition of existing pump foundations is required only to the extent necessary for the installation of new anchor bolts and piping interfaces. All debris generated from the removal of the three existing pumps and associated piping will be hauled to the Oakhaven Regional Landfill, Transfer Station B, in accordance with hazardous waste disposal protocols.

2. UV Sterilization Unit Installation:
   The project includes the installation of a new ultraviolet (UV) sterilization system to enhance water quality treatment standards. This system consists of three parallel treatment banks, each equipped with high-intensity UV lamps and quartz sleeves.
   The new UV units will be installed in a dedicated mechanical room adjacent to the existing pump house. The installation requires minor structural reinforcement of the existing floor slab to support the weight of the equipment and associated fluid reservoirs.
   Piping modifications will be made to the existing water main to integrate the new sterilization units into the flow path. All new piping will be constructed of Schedule 40 PVC, rated for potable water use, and insulated to prevent thermal loss.

3. Equipment Enclosure Construction:
   An above-ground equipment enclosure is required to house the control panels and auxiliary components of the new UV sterilization system.
   Dimensions: 120 square feet (10 feet by 12 feet).
   Height: 14 feet maximum, including roof overhang.
   Material Specification: The enclosure shall be constructed of galvanized steel with a minimum gauge of 12. The exterior finish shall be a high-reflectivity, white enamel coating to minimize heat absorption and reduce the urban heat island effect.
   The enclosure will be bolted to a new concrete pad measuring 12 feet by 14 feet, poured to a depth of 6 inches with a 4-inch layer of compacted gravel sub-base.
   Ventilation: Two passive louvered vents (24 inches by 24 inches each) will be installed on the north and south walls to ensure adequate airflow for the control electronics.
   Security: The enclosure will be fitted with a single-point locking mechanism and a tamper-resistant security door.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION D: ARCHITECTURAL AND ENGINEERING SUMMARIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Architectural Summary:
The proposed construction of the 120 sq. ft. above-ground equipment enclosure is designed to be fully compatible with the existing industrial aesthetic of the North District Water Treatment Plant. The galvanized steel structure will feature a single-pitch roof with a 1/4 inch per foot slope to ensure proper drainage. The roof material shall be standing seam metal, color-matched to the existing facility roofs.
The enclosure will not require any foundation excavation deeper than 18 inches below the existing grade. No changes to the building's exterior lighting or signage are proposed. The structure is classified as an accessory structure and does not require a separate Certificate of Occupancy, provided it remains within the designated utility zone.

Engineering Summary:
The hydraulic design for the new pump configuration has been completed by the Engineering Division. The new pumps are sized to handle a peak flow rate of 4,500 gallons per minute (GPM) each, with a combined maximum capacity of 13,500 GPM.
The piping network has been redesigned to accommodate the new flow dynamics. All new valves will be butterfly valves with pneumatic actuators to allow for remote operation and integration into the central SCADA system.
The electrical load for the new pumps and UV units has been calculated. The total connected load is estimated at 185 kilowatts (kW). The existing electrical service panel at the plant has been verified to have sufficient capacity to accommodate this load with the addition of a new 200-amp feeder circuit.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FROM: Engineering Division
   Dr. Alan K. Sato, Chief Engineer
   1200 Main Street, Suite 410
   Oakhaven, OR 97001

DATE: February 20, 2024
SUBJECT: Hydraulic Modeling for Phase II Upgrades - North District Water Treatment Plant
REFERENCE: Permit Application 2024-P-0214-NW8892
PARCEL ID: NW-8892

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PURPOSE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The purpose of this memorandum is to provide the Engineering Division's analysis regarding the hydraulic modeling results for the proposed North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II. This analysis supports the review of Permit Application 2024-P-0214-NW8892 and informs the Director of Public Works regarding the system impacts of the proposed pump replacement and UV sterilization installation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. BACKGROUND
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The North District Water Treatment Plant (NDWTP) serves a population of approximately 45,000 residents in the northern quadrant of the City of Oakhaven. The current infrastructure, installed in the late 1990s, is approaching the end of its useful life. The Engineering Division has conducted a comprehensive audit of the system, identifying the three high-volume pumps (Units 1, 2, and 3) as critical components requiring immediate replacement to prevent system failure and ensure compliance with the State of Oregon Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) water quality standards.

The proposed project involves the replacement of these three pumps and the installation of new UV sterilization units. The Engineering Division has completed hydraulic modeling simulations to evaluate the impact of the new equipment on system pressure, flow rates, and overall network stability.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. HYDRAULIC MODELING RESULTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The hydraulic model was developed using EPANET 2.2 software, calibrated against field data collected from the NDWTP during the last fiscal year. The model incorporates the proposed pump curves for the new variable-frequency drive (VFD) units and the head loss characteristics of the new piping and UV sterilization system.

3.1 System Pressure Analysis
The primary objective of the Phase II upgrades is to maintain adequate pressure throughout the distribution network during peak usage hours. The modeling results indicate that the new pump configuration will increase system pressure by 15 psi during peak hours (typically between 6:00 AM and 9:00 AM, and 5:00 PM and 8:00 PM).

Current System Pressure:
   Minimum Pressure at Peak Hour: 45 psi
   Maximum Pressure at Peak Hour: 65 psi
   Average Pressure: 55 psi

Projected System Pressure (Post-Upgrade):
   Minimum Pressure at Peak Hour: 60 psi
   Maximum Pressure at Peak Hour: 80 psi
   Average Pressure: 70 psi

The increase of 15 psi is consistent with the design specifications for the new pumps. This pressure increase will ensure that the upper elevations of the North District, which currently experience marginal pressure during peak demand, will receive adequate water service. The modeling also confirms that the pressure will not exceed the maximum allowable limit of 100 psi for the residential distribution pipes, thereby preventing pipe bursts or fitting failures.

3.2 Flow Rate Analysis
The new pumps are designed to operate at a higher efficiency curve than the existing units. The hydraulic model predicts a flow rate increase of approximately 12% during non-peak hours, allowing for more rapid filling of storage tanks. During peak hours, the flow rate will remain within the design capacity of the existing transmission mains, as the increased pressure compensates for the friction losses associated with the new UV sterilization units.

3.3 Energy Consumption
The use of variable-frequency drives on the new pumps allows for precise control of the motor speed based on real-time demand. The modeling indicates a 22% reduction in energy consumption compared to the existing fixed-speed pumps. This reduction will offset a portion of the initial capital investment over the lifecycle of the project.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. EASEMENT AND RIGHT-OF-WAY IMPACTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A critical component of the review process involves the assessment of any changes to existing easements or rights-of-way. The Engineering Division has conducted a site survey and cross-referenced the proposed construction plans with the City's easement database.

Findings:
   1. No changes to existing easements are required for the pump replacement or the UV sterilization installation.
   2. All new equipment will be located within the existing utility footprint of the NDWTP, which is fully enclosed by the current property lines of Parcel ID: NW-8892.
   3. The 120 sq. ft. equipment enclosure will be constructed entirely within the designated equipment pad area, which is already zoned for industrial utility use.
   4. No excavation will extend beyond the 50-foot construction zone boundary established in the environmental review.
   5. No new easements are needed for the installation of the new piping or electrical feeders.

Conclusion:
The project can proceed without the need for any legal modifications to existing easements. The current property boundaries and easement agreements are sufficient to accommodate the proposed infrastructure upgrades.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. BUDGET ALLOCATION AND FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The total estimated cost for the project is $4,250,000. This amount has been allocated from the following funding sources:
   - General Obligation Bond, Series 2023-A: $2,500,000.00
   - Utility Rate Surcharge, Account 9001: $1,250,000.00
   - Reserve Fund, Capital Improvement Program: $500,000.00

The Engineering Division recommends that the budget allocation of $4,250,000 be approved as part of the Capital Improvement Plan (CIP) for Fiscal Year 2024. This allocation includes all costs for equipment, labor, materials, engineering design, and contingency.

5.1 Cost Contingency
A contingency fund of 10% has been included in the total budget to account for unforeseen conditions, such as unexpected soil conditions during excavation or price fluctuations in raw materials (e.g., steel, copper). The Engineering Division advises that this contingency be managed centrally by the Public Works Finance Department.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. RECOMMENDATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Based on the hydraulic modeling and site analysis, the Engineering Division recommends the following:
   1. Approval of the permit application for the replacement of three high-volume pumps and installation of new UV sterilization units at Parcel NW-8892.
   2. Authorization for the construction of the 120 sq. ft. above-ground equipment enclosure constructed of galvanized steel.
   3. Confirmation that no changes to existing easements are required.
   4. Allocation of the budget of $4,250,000 for the project.
   5. Scheduling of the construction phase to begin no earlier than March 1, 2024, to allow for the completion of the environmental review and public notification process.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. ATTACHMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION OFFICE
PRELIMINARY ENVIRONMENTAL ASSESSMENT
PROJECT: North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II
PARCEL ID: NW-8892
DATE OF REVIEW: March 5, 2024
REFERENCE: PERMIT 2024-P-0214-NW8892

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PROJECT DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The City of Oakhaven, through the Oakhaven Utilities Management Group, proposes to undertake infrastructure upgrades at the North District Water Treatment Plant (NDWTP). The project involves the replacement of three high-volume pumps and the installation of new ultraviolet (UV) sterilization units. The scope of work also includes the construction of a 120 sq. ft. above-ground equipment enclosure constructed of galvanized steel. The project is located at Parcel ID: NW-8892, within the I-2 (Heavy Industrial) zoning district.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PURPOSE AND NEED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The purpose of this environmental assessment is to evaluate the potential environmental impacts of the proposed project. The need for the project is driven by the aging condition of the existing water treatment infrastructure, which poses a risk to system reliability and compliance with water quality standards. The upgrades are necessary to ensure the continued delivery of safe, potable water to the residents of the North District.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. METHODOLOGY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The Environmental Protection Office conducted a preliminary assessment based on the following:
   - Review of the permit application submitted on February 14, 2024.
   - Site inspection conducted on February 28, 2024.
   - Analysis of historical environmental data for the NDWTP site.
   - Consultation with the City's Water Quality Division.
   - Review of the Engineering Division's hydraulic modeling report.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ANALYSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4.1 Noise Impact Assessment
Construction activities associated with the replacement of the high-volume pumps and the installation of the UV sterilization units will generate noise. The primary sources of noise include:
   - Demolition of existing pump foundations.
   - Operation of heavy machinery (excavators, cranes, concrete mixers).
   - Installation of new equipment and piping.

The Environmental Protection Office has projected noise levels during the construction phase. Based on the distance of the nearest residential property (approximately 450 feet from the construction zone) and the duration of the work, the noise levels are projected to remain under 65 dBA at the property line. This level is consistent with the City's noise ordinance for industrial construction activities during standard working hours (7:00 AM to 6:00 PM).

No significant noise impacts are anticipated during the operation of the new equipment. The new pumps are equipped with noise-dampening enclosures, and the UV sterilization units are designed to operate quietly. The projected operational noise level is less than 55 dBA.

4.2 Biological Resources and Habitats
The site inspection revealed no wetlands or protected habitats within the 50-foot construction zone. The NDWTP is located in a previously developed industrial area, and the surrounding land has been modified for utility purposes for over two decades. No endangered or threatened species have been identified in the immediate vicinity of the project site.

The 50-foot construction zone boundary was established to ensure a buffer around the existing infrastructure. No vegetation removal is required within this zone, as the area is currently covered with asphalt and gravel. Therefore, no adverse impacts on biological resources are anticipated.

4.3 Water Quality and Waste Water Discharge
The project involves minor modifications to the water treatment process. The installation of the UV sterilization units will enhance the disinfection process, thereby improving water quality. There are no proposed changes to the waste water discharge permits.

The Environmental Protection Office has reviewed the current waste water discharge permit held by the NDWTP. The permit remains in compliance with EPA standards. The construction activities will not introduce new pollutants into the water system. Any wastewater generated during the construction phase (e.g., from concrete washing or equipment cleaning) will be treated and disposed of in accordance with City of Oakhaven waste management protocols.

4.4 Air Quality
Construction activities will generate minor amounts of dust and particulate matter. The Environmental Protection Office requires the contractor to implement dust control measures, including the use of water sprays and the covering of stockpiled materials. The new pumps and UV units are electrically powered and will not generate emissions during operation. The project is not expected to have a significant impact on local air quality.

4.5 Hazardous Materials
The existing pumps and associated piping may contain trace amounts of hazardous materials, such as lead-based paint or asbestos in insulation. The demolition plan includes protocols for the safe removal and disposal of these materials. The contractor, Apex Heavy Industrial Solutions, is required to follow all OSHA and EPA guidelines for hazardous material handling. No new hazardous materials will be introduced to the site.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. MITIGATION MEASURES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
To minimize potential environmental impacts, the following mitigation measures are required:
   1. Noise Control: Construction activities must be limited to standard working hours (7:00 AM to 6:00 PM). Noisier activities, such as jackhammering, are prohibited on weekends and holidays.
   2. Dust Control: The contractor must use water sprays to suppress dust during excavation and demolition.
   3. Waste Management: All construction debris must be hauled to the Oakhaven Regional Landfill, Transfer Station B. Hazardous waste must be disposed of at a licensed facility.
   4. Spill Prevention: A spill containment plan must be in place to prevent the release of fluids (e.g., hydraulic oil, cleaning solvents) into the storm drain system.
   5. Vegetation Protection: No vegetation within the 50-foot construction zone will be removed or damaged.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. CONCLUSIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Based on the preliminary assessment, the proposed project at Parcel NW-8892 will not result in significant adverse environmental impacts. The project is consistent with the City's Comprehensive Plan and the State of Oregon Environmental Policy Act. The construction noise levels are projected to remain under 65 dBA. No wetlands or protected habitats are identified within the 50-foot construction zone. The waste water discharge permit remains in compliance with EPA standards.

The Environmental Protection Office recommends the issuance of the necessary permits, subject to the implementation of the mitigation measures outlined in this report.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. SIGNATURES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Jennifer L. Wu, Environmental Specialist
Environmental Protection Office

Approved by:
Dr. Robert J. Halloway, Director of Public Works

SUBMISSION CONTENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Subject: Concern Regarding Construction Noise at North District Water Treatment Plant

To the Zoning Board and City Council:

I am writing to express my concern regarding the proposed construction of the new pumps and UV sterilization units at the North District Water Treatment Plant. My residence is located approximately 450 feet from the construction site at Parcel NW-8892.

While I understand the necessity of upgrading the water infrastructure, I am concerned about the potential for excessive noise during the construction phase. The preliminary environmental assessment indicates that noise levels may reach 65 dBA. While this may be within legal limits, the cumulative effect of multiple days of construction activity could be disruptive to my household and the surrounding neighborhood.

I respectfully request that the City ensure the following conditions are strictly enforced during the construction of the North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II:
1. Construction work must be limited to the hours of 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday.
2. No work should be conducted on weekends or holidays unless absolutely necessary and approved by the City.
3. The contractor, Apex Heavy Industrial Solutions, must implement all noise mitigation measures outlined in the environmental assessment, including the use of noise barriers and the scheduling of noisy activities (e.g., jackhammering) during the middle of the day.
4. I request a notification system be established to inform nearby residents of any changes to the construction schedule or unexpected noise events.

I am a long-time resident of the North District and have supported the City's efforts to improve water quality. However, I believe that the well-being of the residents should also be a priority during the construction process.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

Thomas R. Baker
3400 Maple Drive
Oakhaven, OR 97001

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION OFFICE
PUBLIC NOTICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW
NOTICE TYPE: Preliminary Environmental Assessment
PROJECT: North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II
PARCEL ID: NW-8892
DATE OF NOTICE: March 5, 2024

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PROJECT DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The City of Oakhaven proposes to undertake infrastructure upgrades at the North District Water Treatment Plant (NDWTP). The project involves the replacement of three high-volume pumps and the installation of new ultraviolet (UV) sterilization units. The scope of work also includes the construction of a 120 sq. ft. above-ground equipment enclosure constructed of galvanized steel. The project is located at Parcel ID: NW-8892, within the I-2 (Heavy Industrial) zoning district.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. PURPOSE AND NEED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The purpose of this environmental assessment is to evaluate the potential environmental impacts of the proposed project. The need for the project is driven by the aging condition of the existing water treatment infrastructure, which poses a risk to system reliability and compliance with water quality standards. The upgrades are necessary to ensure the continued delivery of safe, potable water to the residents of the North District.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A public comment period has been established for this project. The period begins on March 5, 2024, and ends on March 19, 2024.

Members of the public are invited to submit comments regarding the proposed project. Comments may be submitted in writing, via email, or in person at the Zoning and Building Department.

Contact Information for Comments:
   Address: 1200 Main Street, Suite 100, Oakhaven, OR 97001
   Email: comments@oakhaven-city.gov
   Phone: (555) 019-3344

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4.1 Noise Impact
Noise levels during construction are projected to remain under 65 dBA. The Environmental Protection Office has determined that this level is acceptable given the industrial zoning of the site and the distance to the nearest residential properties.

4.2 Biological Resources
No wetlands or protected habitats have been identified within the 50-foot construction zone. The site is located in a previously developed industrial area, and no endangered species are present.

4.3 Water Quality
The waste water discharge permit remains in compliance with EPA standards. The project will not introduce new pollutants into the water system.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. NEXT STEPS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The Zoning Board will review the public comments and the environmental assessment at the meeting scheduled for March 12, 2024. Following the meeting, a final decision will be made regarding the issuance of permits.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. DISTRIBUTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This notice has been distributed to:
   - City Council
   - Zoning and Building Department
   - Environmental Protection Office
   - Local Media Outlets
   - Property Owners within a 1/4 mile radius of Parcel NW-8892

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. SIGNATURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Jennifer L. Wu, Environmental Specialist
Environmental Protection Office
City of Oakhaven
Date: March 5, 2024

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING
DATE: February 25, 2024
SUBJECT: Coordination of Construction Activities at North District Water Treatment Plant

To: Apex Heavy Industrial Solutions
From: Robert J. Halloway, Director of Public Works

This memorandum serves to confirm the coordination of construction activities for the North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II. The following protocols have been established to ensure the safety and efficiency of the project:

1. Work Hours: All construction activities must be conducted between 7:00 AM and 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday. No work is permitted on weekends or holidays without prior written approval from the City.
2. Traffic Management: The contractor must submit a traffic control plan for approval by the City's Transportation Department. All traffic signs and signals must be in place before the start of work.
3. Safety Protocols: All workers must wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) at all times. A safety officer must be present on-site during all construction activities.
4. Waste Disposal: All construction debris must be hauled to the Oakhaven Regional Landfill, Transfer Station B. Hazardous waste must be disposed of at a licensed facility.
5. Communication: The contractor must provide weekly progress reports to the City's Engineering Division. Any changes to the project schedule or scope must be reported immediately.

The contractor agrees to comply with all City of Oakhaven ordinances and regulations during the course of the project.

Signed:
Robert J. Halloway, Director of Public Works
Date: February 25, 2024

APPENDIX B: TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS
================================================================================
CITY OF OAKHAVEN
ENGINEERING DIVISION
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS FOR PUMP REPLACEMENT
PROJECT: North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II
PARCEL ID: NW-8892
DATE: February 20, 2024

1. GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
   - All pumps must be manufactured by a company with a minimum of 20 years of experience in water treatment applications.
   - The pumps must be designed for continuous operation at a flow rate of 4,500 GPM.
   - The pumps must be equipped with variable-frequency drives (VFDs) to allow for precise control of motor speed.

2. MATERIALS
   - Pump Casing: Cast iron, coated with epoxy resin.
   - Impeller: Stainless steel (316L).
   - Shaft: Stainless steel (316L).
   - Bearings: Ball bearings, grease-lubricated.
   - Motor: NEMA Premium Efficiency, 480V, 3-phase.

3. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
   - The pumps must meet the hydraulic performance curves provided in the engineering drawings.
   - The pumps must be tested at the manufacturer's facility before shipment.
   - A performance test must be conducted on-site after installation.

4. INSTALLATION
   - The pumps must be installed by certified technicians.
   - All piping connections must be made using flanged connections.
   - The pumps must be anchored to the concrete foundation using epoxy anchors.

5. MAINTENANCE
   - The pumps must be designed for easy maintenance.
   - All moving parts must be accessible without the need for special tools.
   - A maintenance manual must be provided with each pump.

PROJECT SCOPE: EXPANSION OF EXISTING STORMWATER DETENTION BASIN TO ADD RETENTION CAPACITY.
PROPOSED EXPANSION SIZE: 2.5 ACRES
PROPOSED MATERIALS: HIGH-DENSITY POLYETHYLENE (HDPE) PIPING
PROPOSED PIPING LENGTH: 4,000 LINEAR FEET
TOTAL PROJECTED EXPENDITURE: $1,890,000.00
ESTIMATED COMPLETION DATE: 2024-12-31

TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS
The proposed project involves the modification of the existing stormwater detention infrastructure located within Parcel ID SW-4401. The primary objective is to increase the volumetric retention capacity of the basin to accommodate increased precipitation events within Sector 4.

The expansion will encompass an additional 2.5 acres of retention capacity. This area is adjacent to the existing basin perimeter and is currently designated as undeveloped municipal utility land. The topography of the expansion area slopes at a gradient of 2 percent towards the outflow point. Soil composition analysis indicates a clay-loam mixture with a permeability rating of 0.5 inches per hour.

The piping network will consist of high-density polyethylene (HDPE) piping. The total length of new piping required for the connection of the expansion area to the main collection system is 4,000 linear feet. The piping shall conform to ASTM D3035 standards. The pipe diameter shall be 36 inches for the main collector line and 24 inches for the distribution lines. All joints shall be fused using electrofusion methods to ensure structural integrity and leak prevention.

The structural design includes a concrete weir box at the outlet point to regulate flow rates during high-volume precipitation events. The weir box dimensions are 10 feet by 10 feet by 8 feet deep. The construction will include the installation of inlet structures at 50-foot intervals along the perimeter of the new retention area.

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
OFFICE OF THE CITY MANAGER
INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMORANDUM

TO: DIRECTOR, DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS
FROM: DIRECTOR, DEPARTMENT OF PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT
DATE: 2024-03-15
SUBJECT: COORDINATION OF STORMWATER RETROFIT PROJECT SECTOR 4
REFERENCE: PERMIT APPLICATION SW-2024-0312-A

PURPOSE
This memorandum serves to formalize coordination protocols between the Department of Planning and Development and the Department of Public Works regarding the Stormwater Management System Retrofit project located at Parcel ID SW-4401. This project involves the expansion of the detention basin and the installation of new piping infrastructure by Mid-City Engineering.

COORDINATION REQUIREMENTS
The Department of Public Works is requested to review the proposed excavation plans to ensure no conflicts exist with existing underground utilities. The Department of Planning and Development has received the technical specifications indicating the installation of 4,000 linear feet of HDPE piping. The Department of Public Works shall verify the routing of these lines against the current utility map.

The total projected expenditure of $1,890,000 includes costs for materials, labor, and equipment rental. The budget allocation for this project has been designated under the Capital Improvement Program line item 2024-UTIL-04. The Department of Finance must review the payment schedule to ensure alignment with municipal cash flow requirements.

TIMELINE ALIGNMENT
The proposed construction timeline indicates an anticipated start date of 2024-04-01. The Department of Public Works must confirm availability of road crews to manage traffic control during the excavation phase. Traffic control plans must be submitted to the Department of Public Works no later than 2024-03-25.

The Department of Planning and Development notes that no changes to the approved construction timeline have been approved as of this date. Any variance to the schedule must be submitted in writing and approved by the Zoning Board.

ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE
The Department of Public Works is responsible for monitoring compliance with local environmental regulations during the excavation phase. This includes the management of sediment and erosion control. The Engineering Staff has indicated that silt fence installation protocols will be strictly enforced. This requirement is codified in the municipal stormwater management ordinance Section 14.2.

COMMUNICATION PROTOCOLS
Regular status updates shall be provided on a bi-weekly basis. The primary point of contact for the Department of Public Works shall be the Senior Utilities Engineer. The primary point of contact for the Department of Planning and Development shall be the Permitting Coordinator.

All correspondence regarding this project shall reference Permit Application Number SW-2024-0312-A and Parcel ID SW-4401.

APPROVAL STATUS
This memorandum is issued for informational purposes and does not constitute final approval of the construction plans. Final approval is contingent upon the Zoning Board vote scheduled for 2024-03-20.

OPENING REMARKS

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
OFFICE OF SUSTAINABILITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE
ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY

PROJECT TITLE: STORMWATER MANAGEMENT SYSTEM RETROFIT - SECTOR 4
PROJECT LOCATION: PARCEL ID SW-4401
DATE OF REVIEW: 2024-03-18
REVIEW TYPE: CLASS II REVIEW
REVIEW STATUS: COMPLETE

SUMMARY OF REVIEW
This Environmental Review Summary documents the compliance assessment of the proposed Stormwater Management System Retrofit project. The review was conducted in accordance with the City of Oakhaven Environmental Protection Ordinance. The project involves the expansion of a detention basin and the installation of new infrastructure.

SCREENING CRITERIA
The following screening criteria were evaluated:
1. Air Quality: The construction phase involves heavy machinery. Emissions are within state limits. No permanent air quality impact is expected.
2. Water Quality: The project involves stormwater management. The design includes filtration measures. No discharge of pollutants is anticipated.
3. Biological Resources: The site is designated as utility land. No critical habitat is present.
4. Noise: Construction noise will occur during daylight hours. Noise mitigation measures are included in the permit conditions.
5. Traffic: Construction traffic will be managed via the traffic control plan submitted by Mid-City Engineering.

SPECIFIC FINDINGS
The project adds 2.5 acres of retention capacity. This expansion does not increase the impervious surface area of the surrounding residential zones. The installation of 4,000 linear feet of HDPE piping is contained within the utility corridor.
The total projected expenditure of $1,890,000 includes environmental mitigation costs.
The project does not require a full Environmental Impact Statement. A Class II Review is sufficient.

MITIGATION MEASURES
The following mitigation measures are required:
1. Installation of silt fences along the perimeter of the excavation site.
2. Dust control measures during dry weather.
3. Erosion control matting on disturbed soil.
4. Sediment traps at all outlet points.

CONSULTATION
The Department of Public Works was consulted regarding the impact on existing utilities. The Department of Health was consulted regarding water quality. No adverse findings were reported by either department.

CONCLUSION
Based on the review, the project is compliant with the City of Oakhaven Environmental Protection Ordinance. The project does not present significant adverse environmental impacts. Approval is recommended subject to the implementation of the mitigation measures listed above.

DOCUMENT 1: BOARD MINUTES - SPECIAL SESSION (MILLBROOK)

CITY OF CITY CENTER
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
SPECIAL SESSION MINUTES

DATE: July 19, 2024
TIME: 6:00 PM TO 8:00 PM
LOCATION: City Hall Room 204
MEETING ID: ZBA-07-19
RECORDER: Administrative Assistant

CALL TO ORDER
The Special Session was called to order by Chairperson Marcus Patterson at 6:00 PM. A quorum was established with the presence of seven (7) members. The following members were present and accounted for: Marcus Patterson, Elena Kowalski, David Rivera, Sarah Chen, Thompson, Martinez, and Wilson. The Zoning Board of Appeals convened for the purpose of hearing applications regarding zoning variances and land use requests.

AGENDA ITEM 1: APPLICATION 24-011
Applicant: Millbrook Community Land Trust
Property Address: 1600 Community Drive
Request Type: Setback Variance
Zoning District: R-1
Variance Request: 24-011

The Board considered the Land Trust application. Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20 was reviewed by the Board members. Staff Recommendation: APPROVE was noted in the official file. The application seeks a variance regarding the minimum setback requirements for the proposed affordable housing project.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The Board opened the floor for public comment. Public Speakers: 15 individuals registered to speak. Comments were recorded in the Public Comment Log attached to these minutes. Speakers included residents of the immediate vicinity, representatives of the Millbrook Community Land Trust, and members of the City Planning Department.

STAFF PRESENTATION
The Planning Department representative presented the technical findings regarding the variance request. The staff report highlighted the need for the variance to accommodate the design of the affordable housing units. The staff noted that the current zoning district R-1 allows for residential development but specifies strict setback lines.

BOARD DISCUSSION
Members discussed the implications of the variance. Discussion focused on the technical specifications of the property lines. The Board considered the technicality cited regarding the boundary alignment.

STATEMENT OF TECHNICALITY
Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant with the current code interpretation. The Board reviewed the survey data submitted by the applicant. The data indicated a discrepancy in the measured distance from the property line to the proposed structure.

MOTION AND VOTE
Motion to deny was made by Member Patterson. Seconded by Member Kowalski. The motion was placed before the Board for voting.

ROLL CALL VOTE
The Clerk called the roll for the vote on Motion 24-011-Deny.
Roll Call Vote: Yes (3)
Roll Call Vote: No (4)
Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0)

The Yes Voters were identified as Thompson, Martinez, Wilson. The No Voters were identified as Patterson, Kowalski, Rivera, Chen. The vote was 4-3 to deny. The Motion Carried.

ORDER ISSUED
Order Number: ORD-2024-19
Order Signed: July 19, 2024
Distribution: Applicant, City Clerk

The Board issued an Order denying the variance request. The denial was based on the finding that the setback calculation did not meet the minimum requirements of the Municipal Code. The Order states that the applicant may submit a revised application or appeal the decision.

APPEAL PROCESS
Appeal Deadline: July 19, 2025
Applicant Response: Pending
The Millbrook Community Land Trust was notified of the Appeal Deadline. The Applicant Response is currently Pending as of the time of this recording.

PROJECT DETAILS
Project Type: Affordable Housing
Unit Count: 40
Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99
Grant Impact: Contingency at Risk
Federal Grant: Contingent

The project is designated as Affordable Housing with a Unit Count of 40 units. A Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99 is associated with the funding for this development. The Grant Impact is noted as Contingency at Risk due to the variance denial. The Federal Grant status is Contingent upon the resolution of the zoning variance.

COMMUNITY AND MEDIA
Community Reaction: Strong Opposition
Media Coverage: Local News

The Community Reaction is recorded as Strong Opposition to the variance. Media Coverage: Local News reported on the proceedings of the meeting. The local news outlet covered the vote count and the specific details of the denial.

NEXT STEPS
Next Meeting: July 22, 2024
Location: City Hall Room 204
Contact: ZBA Clerk

The Board adjourned the session at 8:00 PM. The minutes were recorded and are subject to approval at the next meeting.

DOCUMENT 2: BOARD MINUTES - SPECIAL SESSION (CRESTVIEW)

CITY OF CITY CENTER
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
SPECIAL SESSION MINUTES

DATE: July 19, 2024
TIME: 6:00 PM TO 8:00 PM
LOCATION: City Hall Room 204
MEETING ID: ZBA-07-19-2
RECORDER: Administrative Assistant

CALL TO ORDER
The Special Session continued for the second agenda item. The Board convened at 6:00 PM and adjourned at 8:00 PM. Quorum Established. The following members were present: Marcus Patterson, Elena Kowalski, David Rivera, Sarah Chen, Thompson, Martinez, and Wilson.

AGENDA ITEM 2: APPLICATION 24-010
Applicant: Crestview Holdings LLC
Property Address: 1500 River Road
Request Type: Variance for Density
Variance Request: 24-010

The Board considered the Crestview Holdings application. Staff Report ID: PD-2024-07-10 was reviewed by the Board members. Staff Recommendation: DENY was noted in the official file by the Planning Department. The application seeks a variance for density regarding the proposed multi-unit development.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The Board opened the floor for public comment. Public Speakers: 10 individuals registered to speak. Comments were recorded in the Public Comment Log attached to these minutes. Speakers included local business owners, residents, and representatives of the Crestview Holdings LLC.

STAFF PRESENTATION
The Planning Department representative presented the findings regarding the density variance. The staff noted insufficient parking documentation. The staff report indicated that the parking plan submitted did not meet the requirements for the proposed unit count.

BOARD DISCUSSION
Members discussed the parking requirements and the density of the proposed structure. Discussion focused on the infrastructure capacity of the surrounding area. The Board considered the implications of the density increase on local traffic patterns.

STATEMENT OF FINDINGS
Staff noted insufficient parking documentation. The Board reviewed the parking submission forms. The forms lacked the required number of spaces per unit as mandated by the Municipal Code.

MOTION AND VOTE
Motion to approve variance 24-010 was made. Seconded by Member Rivera. The motion was placed before the Board for voting.

ROLL CALL VOTE
The Clerk called the roll for the vote on Motion 24-010-Approve.
Roll Call Vote: Yes (4)
Roll Call Vote: No (3)
Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0)

The Yes Voters were identified as Patterson, Kowalski, Rivera, Chen. The No Voters were identified as Thompson, Martinez, Wilson. The vote was 4-3 to approve. The Motion Carried.

ORDER ISSUED
Order Number: ORD-2024-19-2
Order Signed: July 19, 2024
Distribution: Applicant, City Clerk

The Board issued an Order approving the variance request. The approval allows the applicant to proceed with the density variance subject to conditions.

APPEAL PROCESS
Appeal Deadline: July 19, 2025
Applicant Response: None

The Appeal Deadline is July 19, 2025. Applicant Response: None was recorded at the time of the meeting.

Project Approved: Yes
Units Approved: 200
Zoning Change: Effective

The Variance Approved status is Yes. The Project Approved status is Yes. The Units Approved is 200 units. The Zoning Change is Effective immediately upon the filing of the Order.

SCOPE OF REVIEW
The Environmental Protection Division conducted a review of the environmental impact statements submitted for the two variance requests. The review covered soil stability, water drainage, noise levels, and air quality.

PROJECT 1 REVIEW (MILLBROOK)
Property: 1600 Community Drive
Zoning District: R-1
Project Type: Affordable Housing
Unit Count: 40

Soil Analysis:
The soil analysis indicates stable ground conditions. No hazardous materials were detected in the subsurface testing. The soil composition is suitable for residential foundation construction.
Water Drainage:
The water drainage plan meets the municipal standards for runoff management. The proposed drainage system will direct water to the existing storm sewer system.
Noise Levels:
Noise levels are projected to be within acceptable limits for residential zones. The distance from the nearest highway ensures minimal noise pollution.
Air Quality:
Air quality monitoring shows no significant increase in particulate matter. The construction phase will follow dust control protocols.
Environmental Impact:
The environmental impact is considered Low. Mitigation measures are in place for the construction phase.

DOCUMENT 2.0: ARCHITECTURAL AND ENGINEERING SUMMARY

CITY OF OAKHAVEN DEPARTMENT OF PLANNING
TECHNICAL REVIEW MEMORANDUM

FILE REFERENCE: UTIL-2024-003
DOCUMENT DATE: 2024-04-02
PREPARED BY: Sarah J. Chen, Senior Architectural Reviewer

SUBJECT: Architectural Plans Review for Substation B Upgrade

1.0 OVERVIEW

This memorandum summarizes the architectural review of the proposed modifications to Substation B, Parcel ID: EL-3310. The review was conducted in accordance with the City of Oakhaven Zoning Ordinance Chapter 14, Section 14.5 regarding Utility Infrastructure Facilities. The project involves the replacement of existing electrical equipment with modernized units to accommodate increased demand.

2.0 SITE LAYOUT AND SETBACKS

The proposed site layout maintains existing property lines. No new structures are being added outside the current perimeter fence. The new equipment footprint is identical to the previous equipment footprint.
Perimeter Fence: Existing 8-foot chain link fencing remains in place.
Access Road: Existing gravel access road at the rear of the property remains unchanged.
Setback Requirements: The project complies with the minimum 10-foot setback requirement for utility structures from adjacent residential properties.

3.0 STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS

New transformers rated at 15 MVA to support growing commercial load.
Weight Distribution: The new units weigh approximately 12,000 pounds each.
Foundation: Concrete slab foundation with 4-inch thickness.
Anchor Bolts: High-tensile steel anchors securing equipment to foundation.
Seismic Rating: Equipment meets Seismic Zone 4 requirements as defined by the California Building Code (adopted by reference).

4.0 NOISE CONTROL MEASURES

Architectural plans show noise-reducing acoustic barriers around the new equipment.
Barrier Height: 12 feet total height.
Material Composition: Pre-cast concrete panels with internal acoustic foam lining.
Sound Attenuation Rating: NRC 0.85.
Expected Noise Level at Property Line: 45 dBA.
Background Noise Level at Property Line: 50 dBA.
Compliance: The design ensures sound levels do not exceed local noise ordinances.

5.0 MATERIALS AND FINISHES

Exterior Finish: Industrial grade epoxy paint, color RAL 7035 (Light Grey).
Roofing: Flat roof with metal flashing.
Drainage: Internal drainage system directs rainwater to existing storm sewer.
Fencing: Chain link with privacy slats installed on the north and west sides to screen equipment from view.

6.0 ELECTRICAL CONNECTIONS

High Voltage Connection: 138 kV transmission line.
Low Voltage Connection: 13.8 kV distribution line.
Grounding: 100% copper grounding grid installed.
Disconnect Switches: Manual disconnect switches installed at ground level for safety.

7.0 CONSTRUCTION ACCESS

Construction vehicles will enter from Powerline Road.
Designated Parking Area: South-west corner of the site.
Signage: Standard construction safety signage required.
Lighting: Temporary floodlights permitted for night work.

8.0 REVIEW COMMENTS

The review team notes the following items for contractor attention:
1. Ensure all temporary fencing is secured before work begins.
2. Verify grounding continuity prior to energization.
3. Submit final as-built drawings upon project completion.

This review does not constitute approval of the electrical work. Electrical permit required from Electrical Division.

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
OFFICE OF THE MAYOR
INTERNAL MEMORANDUM

DATE: 2024-04-10
TO: City Council
FROM: Electrical Operations
RE: Grid redundancy improvements

1.0 SUMMARY

This memorandum outlines the scope and impact of the Substation B Upgrade Project. The objective is to enhance the reliability of the electrical grid within the municipal boundaries.

2.0 PROJECT OBJECTIVES

The primary objective of the Substation B upgrade is to improve grid redundancy. Substation B upgrade will reduce outage probability by 40% in the downtown district. This improvement is necessary to accommodate the increased load generated by new commercial developments in the central business district.

3.0 OPERATIONAL DETAILS

Maintenance shutdown scheduled for 02:00-06:00 on April 15.
Affected Areas: Downtown District, Sector 4, Sector 5.
Duration: 4 Hours.
Notification: Customers will receive email and SMS notification 48 hours in advance.
Emergency Contact: 555-019-9999 (24-hour dispatch).

4.0 PROPERTY AND LAND USE

No property acquisition required.
Existing Easements: The project utilizes existing utility easements recorded in Volume 45, Page 12.
Right of Way: No encroachment into public right of way.
Land Use: The land use remains consistent with the current U-2 zoning designation.

5.0 BUDGET AND FUNDING

Total Project Budget: $3,100,000.
Source of Funds: Municipal Capital Improvement Fund.
Allocation:
- Equipment Procurement: $2,100,000
- Labor and Installation: $750,000
- Permits and Fees: $150,000
- Contingency: $100,000

6.0 SAFETY AND RISK MANAGEMENT

Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): Required for all personnel.
Lockout/Tagout: Procedures to be followed in accordance with OSHA 1910.147.
Environmental Safety: Spill containment kits required on-site.
Insurance: General Liability and Workers Compensation coverage current.

7.0 COORDINATION WITH OTHER DEPARTMENTS

Public Works: Coordination required for traffic control during shutdown.
Police Department: Notification for patrol awareness.
Fire Department: Notification for hazard awareness.
Planning Department: Review of zoning compliance completed.

8.0 CONCLUSION

The Substation B upgrade represents a critical investment in municipal infrastructure. Implementation will proceed according to the schedule outlined above. Further updates will be provided upon completion.

DOCUMENT 4.0: ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY

CITY OF OAKHAVEN DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY
ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY

FILE REFERENCE: UTIL-2024-003
DOCUMENT DATE: 2024-04-12
PROJECT: Substation B Upgrade
PARCEL ID: EL-3310

1.0 INTRODUCTION

This Environmental Review Summary documents the findings of the environmental assessment conducted for the Substation B Upgrade. The review was performed in accordance with the City of Oakhaven Environmental Impact Policy (EIP) Section 8.1.

2.0 ELECTROMAGNETIC FIELD (EMF) ANALYSIS

Electromagnetic field (EMF) analysis for Substation B was conducted by Third Party Environmental Consultants, LLC.
Methodology: Field measurements taken at 1-meter intervals around the perimeter.
Frequency Range: 50 Hz to 60 Hz.
Measurement Equipment: Triaxial Gaussmeter Model 3000.
Results: Projected fields at property line remain well below FCC safety limits.
Comparison:
- Measured Field Strength: 0.5 Gauss.
- FCC Safety Limit: 2.0 Gauss.
- Margin of Safety: 75%.

3.0 SOIL AND GROUNDWATER ASSESSMENT

Soil contamination tests prior to excavation returned negative results.
Sample Points: 4 locations (North, South, East, West).
Depth: 0 to 6 feet.
Contaminants Tested: PCBs, Heavy Metals, Volatile Organic Compounds.
Results: All samples below detection limits.
Groundwater Depth: 15 feet below surface.
Aquifer Protection: No impact on drinking water sources expected.

4.0 CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS

Construction materials are 100% recyclable.
Materials List:
- Transformer Oil: Recyclable synthetic fluid.
- Steel Enclosures: Recycled steel content 85%.
- Concrete: Reclaimed aggregate 20%.
- Wiring: Copper (100% recyclable).
Disposal Plan: All waste materials to be transported to certified recycling facility.

5.0 WASTE MANAGEMENT

Construction Waste:
- Packaging: Cardboard and Plastic to be separated.
- Debris: Metal scrap to be recycled.
- Hazardous Waste: None anticipated.
Spill Prevention: Secondary containment for all liquid storage.

6.0 NOISE AND AIR QUALITY

Noise: Acoustic barriers will reduce operational noise.
Air Quality: No emissions expected during operation.
Dust Control: Water trucks to be used during excavation.
Traffic: Construction vehicles to follow designated routes.

7.0 REGULATORY COMPLIANCE

Compliance with State Environmental Protection Agency (SEPA) Guidelines.
Compliance with Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Regulations.
Compliance with National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) where applicable.

8.0 RECOMMENDATIONS

1. Proceed with construction as planned.
2. Monitor noise levels during initial operation.
3. Submit annual environmental compliance report.

SECTION 1: ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS MEETING MINUTES
================================================================================

**CITY OF MILLBROOK**
**ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS**
**REGULAR MEETING MINUTES**

**Date:** July 19, 2024
**Time:** 6:00 PM – 9:15 PM
**Location:** City Hall, Room 101, 500 Industrial Way, Millbrook, State 12345
**Presiding Officer:** Chairperson Robert J. Miller
**Clerk:** Sarah J. Miller (Deputy)

**I. ROLL CALL**

The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM. The following members were present and counted for a quorum:
1. Robert J. Miller (Chairperson)
2. Elena Rodriguez (Vice-Chairperson)
3. David Thompson (Member)
4. Marcus Webb (Member)
5. Sarah Chen (Member)

The following members were absent: None.
The following staff were present:
- Planning Director: James H. Patterson
- Zoning Administrator: Linda K. Foster
- Legal Counsel: Attorney Thomas A. Smith
- Public Information Officer: Karen L. Davis

**II. CALL TO ORDER AND PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE**

Chairperson Miller called the meeting to order. The Pledge of Allegiance was recited by the assembled members and staff.

**III. APPROVAL OF PREVIOUS MEETING MINUTES**

Motion was made by Member Thompson, seconded by Member Rodriguez, to approve the minutes of the meeting held on June 21, 2024.
Discussion: No discussion was required.
Vote:
- Aye: Miller, Rodriguez, Thompson, Webb, Chen (5)
- Nay: 0
- Abstain: 0
The minutes were approved as submitted.

**IV. PUBLIC HEARING: VARIANCE REQUEST FOR CRESTVIEW HOLDINGS LLC**

**A. Introduction of Matter**

Planning Director Patterson introduced the agenda item regarding the application filed by Crestview Holdings LLC (hereinafter "Applicant") for a Variance Request to rezone Parcel ID 44-092-18 from R-1 (Single Family Residential) to C-2 (General Commercial) to facilitate the construction of a mixed-use commercial complex. The Applicant was represented by legal counsel for the firm of Webb & Associates.

**B. Staff Report and Recommendations**

Zoning Administrator Foster presented the staff report. The report noted that the proposed development, to be known as the "Millbrook Industrial Plaza," would consist of 45,000 square feet of retail and office space. The staff recommendation was to approve the variance, subject to specific conditions regarding traffic mitigation and landscaping, noting that the proposed use aligns with the City's Comprehensive Plan for the Industrial Corridor.

**C. Public Comment Period**

The Chairperson opened the public comment period.

1. **Comment 1:** Mr. Arthur P. Henderson, resident of 124 Oak Street, addressed the board. Mr. Henderson expressed concern regarding the increase in traffic volume on Industrial Way. He stated, "This development will overwhelm our local intersections. The traffic studies provided do not account for peak hour congestion during the school year."

2. **Comment 2:** Mrs. Elena G. Rossi, owner of a local boutique on Main Street, spoke in favor of the project. She noted, "New commercial space is necessary for the economic vitality of Millbrook. I hope the board approves this to ensure we retain local businesses."

3. **Comment 3:** Ms. Sarah Chen, ZBA Member, noted that she had received a disclosure form regarding a potential conflict of interest but had not yet filed it as the contract was still in the drafting phase. The Chairperson noted that the disclosure would be filed immediately following the vote.

4. **Comment 4:** Mr. Marcus Webb, ZBA Member, addressed the board. He stated, "I have reviewed the financial disclosures and the ownership structure as presented by the Applicant. I find the information satisfactory for the purpose of this hearing."

**D. Board Deliberation**

The Board engaged in a discussion regarding the traffic mitigation plan.
- Member Thompson asked for clarification on the proposed off-street parking ratio.
- Planning Director Patterson responded that the ratio would be 1 space per 300 square feet, exceeding the standard requirement.
- Member Rodriguez inquired about the environmental impact assessment.
- Legal Counsel Smith confirmed that the environmental review had been completed and found no significant adverse impacts.

**E. Motion and Vote**

Chairperson Miller called for a motion to grant the Variance Request.

**Motion:** Member Thompson moved to approve the Variance Request for Crestview Holdings LLC, Parcel ID 44-092-18, with the conditions set forth in the staff report regarding traffic, landscaping, and soundproofing.
**Second:** Member Rodriguez.

**Discussion on Recusal:**
Chairperson Miller asked Member Chen if she wished to recuse herself from the vote.
Member Chen stated, "I do not wish to recuse myself at this time as I have not yet received a signed contract or payment, though I have been notified of a pending agreement."
Chairperson Miller asked Member Webb if he wished to recuse himself.
Member Webb stated, "I do not wish to recuse myself."

**Roll Call Vote:**
The Chairperson called for a roll-call vote on Motion 2024-07-19-A.

1. **Robert J. Miller:** Aye.
2. **Elena Rodriguez:** Aye.
3. **David Thompson:** Aye.
4. **Marcus Webb:** Aye.
5. **Sarah Chen:** Aye.

**Result:** The motion passed by a vote of 5-0.

**F. Adjournment**

The Chairperson declared the public hearing closed.
Motion was made by Member Thompson, seconded by Member Rodriguez, to adjourn the meeting.
The meeting was adjourned at 9:15 PM.

**Respectfully Submitted,**
Linda K. Foster, Zoning Administrator

**CITY OF MILLBROOK**
**DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION**
**ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY**
**DOCUMENT ID:** ENV-2024-07-22-CREST

**Project:** Millbrook Industrial Plaza
**Applicant:** Crestview Holdings LLC
**Parcel ID:** 44-092-18
**Review Date:** June 28, 2024
**Review Officer:** Dr. Emily J. Carter, Environmental Scientist

**I. SCOPE OF REVIEW**

This review assesses the potential environmental impacts of the proposed construction and operation of the Millbrook Industrial Plaza. The scope includes air quality, water quality, noise pollution, traffic, and waste management.

**II. AIR QUALITY**

**Assessment:** The construction phase will involve the use of heavy machinery, which may generate dust and emissions.
**Mitigation:** The applicant has committed to using low-emission equipment and implementing dust control measures, including regular watering of the site and covering stockpiles.
**Conclusion:** No significant adverse impact on air quality is anticipated.

**III. WATER QUALITY**

**Assessment:** The project involves the construction of a new parking lot and building footprint.
**Mitigation:** The applicant has designed a stormwater management system that includes a retention basin and permeable pavement. The system is designed to handle the 100-year storm event.
**Conclusion:** No significant adverse impact on water quality is anticipated.

**IV. NOISE POLLUTION**

**Assessment:** The proposed commercial use will generate noise during construction and operation.
**Mitigation:** The applicant has proposed noise barriers along the rear boundary and the use of soundproofing materials for the building.
**Conclusion:** Noise levels will remain within acceptable limits as per City Ordinance 2023-15.

**V. TRAFFIC IMPACT**

**Assessment:** The project will increase traffic volume on Industrial Way.
**Mitigation:** The applicant has proposed a dedicated right-turn lane and improved signal timing at the intersection of Industrial Way and Oak Street.
**Conclusion:** Traffic impacts are manageable with the proposed mitigation measures.

**VI. WASTE MANAGEMENT**

**Assessment:** The construction phase will generate debris.
**Mitigation:** The applicant has committed to recycling 75% of construction debris and using a licensed waste disposal facility.
**Conclusion:** No significant adverse impact on waste management is anticipated.

**VII. OVERALL CONCLUSION**

Based on the review, the proposed project complies with the City's Environmental Quality Standards. No significant adverse impacts were identified. The project is recommended for approval, subject to the conditions set forth in the staff report.

**Signature:**
Dr. Emily J. Carter
Environmental Scientist
